explanation.summary: Proteins are carrying this food most, while minerals are the biggest drag. It lands in S tier.
explanation.whyThisTier: This food lands in S tier with an overall score of 64 from a base nutrition score of 64. Context barely changed the result.

topPros.title: one of the cheaper ways to buy useful nutrition
topPros.explanation: That matters for real diets, not just theory.

topPros.title: does a lot of jobs at once
topPros.explanation: Protein, fibre, and meal utility are why legumes stay valuable.

topPros.title: filling for the calories
topPros.explanation: Legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety.

topCons.title: texture is not universally loved
topCons.explanation: That can reduce how often people actually use them.

topCons.title: can take planning or prep
topCons.explanation: That is a small but real friction cost.

topCons.title: prep time can still be a barrier
topCons.explanation: Dry legumes especially ask more from the cook.

closing.summary: Kidney Beans has a genuinely strong overall case. the practical downsides are real, but they do not outweigh the upside. best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.useCaseNote: Best when you want fibre, protein, and actual meal utility together.
closing.cta: Would you rank it the same, or nah?

Review narration for category repetition and trim any awkward lines.
Confirm subtitle density stays readable at phone speed.
Replace placeholder hero/sprite art with final asset selection.
Check that the final tier verdict feels editorially defensible.
nextHumanStep: Review the generated narration + manifest, then use the manifest as the handoff into visual assembly / editing.
